seed: restlessness vs peace; memory_vs_motion; diagonal_argument; edge_crawl; flame against shatter; closed_corners; light_vs_dark; patch; signal_noise | abstract variation request 167; target batch count 500
title: Hunger vs Restraint
reasoning: This piece reflects the tension of 'restlessness vs peace' by employing a dynamic palette where vibrant hues collide with dark tones, accentuating the push and pull of emotion. The selection of hot coral and blood orange suggests a fervent energy, while hollow blue and charcoal introduce a grounding silence, representing the dichotomy between desire and calm. The central area is saturated, embodying an overfilled core that visually disrupts the edges, creating a sense of confinement. This layout encapsulates the struggle of memory against motion through echo-like patterns, aligning with the seed's exploration of contrasting emotions and boundaries. The interaction of colors creates a rhythmic flicker, enhancing the feeling of unsettled borders, while intentional broken pixels introduce imperfections, symbolizing the scars left by memories.

layout.name: overfilled_center
layout.description: The center is dense and saturated while the border struggles to contain it.

composition.name: hunger_vs_restraint
composition.idea: A saturated pull reaches toward a locked border that refuses expansion.

referenceExample.title: Signal Inside Noise
referenceExample.pattern: one readable green path repeatedly interrupted by magenta static

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
